title: linaria × sepium subsp. ochroleuca
intro: Linaria × sepium subsp. ochroleuca, also known as the Toadflax, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the figwort family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and green foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Linaria × sepium subsp. ochroleuca include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
